frame_analyses.description: Equipment setup shown on bench. Instruments visible including needle drivers, scissors, and suture block with marked Penrose drain visible.
Close-up of knot pusher instrument being shown
Penrose drain with marked targets illustrated with overlaid annotation 'A' marking one target
Two suture blocks (AuroSuture training boxes) shown from above
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS extracorporeal knot training setup

frame_analyses.description: Hands demonstrating proper grasp of Penrose drain with suture block anchoring
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows stabilization technique for drain during suturing

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic view entering the laparoscopic trainer; needle and suture visible with instrument approaching target area on simulated tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First instrument insertion shown; needle approach technique

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver advancing needle through marked target on foam tissue. Entry appears perpendicular and on-mark.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle entry at appropriate angle

frame_analyses.description: Needle exiting first target, suture thread trailing behind. Position appears accurate to marked line.
Second pass: needle driver repositioning for second target entry. Needle oriented for backhand approach.
Needle entering second marked target. Entry appears on-line, perpendicular approach maintained.
Demonstration of extracorporeal knot tying outside the trainer box. Hands manipulating suture ends to form first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows proper hand positioning for knot formation outside box

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher being advanced along suture to push completed knot down toward drain. Smooth advancement visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper knot pusher technique and tension application

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of completed suture block with drain showing secured knot. Slit in drain appears closed with good approximation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final result demonstration shows complete slit closure

video_type_notes: This is a comprehensive instructional/educational video demonstrating proper FLS Task 4 (Extracorporeal Suture Knot) technique. The video includes equipment setup, anatomical target identification, step-by-step demonstration of needle passes through marked targets, extracorporeal knot formation, and knot pusher advancement. There is narration/educational context (evidenced by Memorial University watermark and Krisztina Bajzak name, suggesting this is a teaching aid). The video does not show a trainee under timed conditions—it is an instructional demonstration.
scoring_rationale: This video is classified as 'instructional' per the assessment rubric. Instructional/tutorial content with educational narration, clear teaching intent, and demonstration of proper technique should NOT be scored as a performance. Instead, teaching content has been extracted below. No completion time, penalties, or performance score are applicable.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper needle loading at optimal grip point (approximately 2/3 from tip)
Perpendicular needle entry through marked targets on Penrose drain
Appropriate angle adjustment between first and second target passes
Backhand needle repositioning for second pass
Hands-on stabilization of drain/suture block during needle placement
Extracorporeal knot formation outside the trainer box using manual hand technique
Smooth advancement of knot pusher along suture to apply tension
Proper tension management to achieve complete slit closure in drain
Identification and alignment with marked target lines before needle entry
verbal_cues: Video includes educational context and proper technique demonstration but specific verbal narration is not transcribable from the video frames
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding off-target needle entry by maintaining perpendicular approach
Preventing suture deviation by careful alignment with marked lines before passing needle
Ensuring adequate knot tension for complete slit approximation
Proper knot pusher technique to avoid knot slippage during advancement
key_learning_points: Target marks must be clearly identified before needle approach
Needle entry must be perpendicular and on-line with marked targets
Extracorporeal knot formation allows for better visualization and control
Knot pusher must advance smoothly without hesitation to maintain tension
Complete slit closure is the end-state objective

cannot_determine: Completion time — instructional video, not timed performance
Performance penalties — this is a demonstration, not a trainee attempt
Task difficulty classification for this individual — no trainee performance visible
confidence_rationale: Video classification is definitive: this is clearly an instructional/educational demonstration produced by Memorial University with labeled watermarks and professional setup. It is not a trainee performance under exam conditions. All frames clearly show step-by-step teaching content rather than real-time task execution. No scoring is appropriate.
